codeReview.summary: The feature commit correctly adds deployed-environment validation evidence for both required assertions (VAL-CROSS-004 and VAL-CROSS-005) on the actual Vercel URL, including route refresh checks for all required valid non-root routes and HTTP 404 evidence for unknown routes. No blocking correctness, security, or reliability defects were found for feature scope.

issues.description: Both unknown-route evidence entries record `notFoundMain: false` (lines 9 and 22), so the custom not-found-container signal did not validate positively even though the assertion is marked pass. The pass is still supported by title/h1/articleCount and 404 status evidence, but this field currently weakens validation clarity.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Deploy-evidence flow artifacts embed machine-specific absolute Windows screenshot paths, while earlier flow artifacts use portable relative evidence paths; shared guidance does not define a canonical evidence-path format.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Absolute paths appear in `.factory/validation/deploy-evidence/user-testing/flows/deployed-cross-routes.json` lines 38-47 and 73-74; existing relative-path convention appears in `.factory/validation/blog-and-deploy/user-testing/flows/cross-area.json` lines 55-56, 116, and 152-153.

summary: Pass with one non-blocking validation-quality issue. The deployed Vercel evidence coverage matches the feature requirements, but unknown-route UI evidence includes a contradictory `notFoundMain: false` signal and path-format consistency should be standardized.
